name: ExtensionSubscriptionTopic_CanFilterBy
title: R5: Properties by which a Subscription can filter notifications from the SubscriptionTopic (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `SubscriptionTopic.canFilterBy` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`SubscriptionTopic.canFilterBy` 0..* `BackboneElement`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `SubscriptionTopic.canFilterBy` 0..* `BackboneElement`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `SubscriptionTopic.canFilterBy` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `SubscriptionTopic` is not mapped.
Element `SubscriptionTopic.canFilterBy` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: URL of the triggering Resource that this filter applies to (new)
element.comment:

URL of the Resource that is the type used in this filter. Relative URLs are relative to the StructureDefinition root of the implemented FHIR version (e.g., http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition). For example, "Patient" maps to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Patient. For more information, see [ElementDefinition.type.code](https://hl7.org/fhir/elementdefinition-definition.html#ElementDefinition.type.code).

element.short: URL of the triggering Resource that this filter applies to
element.definition: URL of the Resource that is the type used in this filter. This is the "focus" of the topic (or one of them if there are more than one). It will be the same, a generality, or a specificity of SubscriptionTopic.resourceTrigger.resource or SubscriptionTopic.eventTrigger.resource when they are present.
element.comment: URL of the Resource that is the type used in this filter. Relative URLs are relative to the StructureDefinition root of the implemented FHIR version (e.g., http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition). For example, "Patient" maps to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Patient. For more information, see [ElementDefinition.type.code](https://hl7.org/fhir/elementdefinition-definition.html#ElementDefinition.type.code).

binding.description: A type of resource, or a Reference (from all versions)

element.short: R5: Human-readable and computation-friendly name for a filter parameter usable by subscriptions on this topic, via Subscription.filterBy.filterParameter (new)
element.comment:
Element `SubscriptionTopic.canFilterBy.filterParameter` is part of an existing definition because parent element `SubscriptionTopic.canFilterBy` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `SubscriptionTopic.canFilterBy.filterParameter` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `SubscriptionTopic` is not mapped.
Element `SubscriptionTopic.canFilterBy.filterParameter` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Chained parameters are allowed (like "patient.gender") - but can not use colons or modifiers.
element.requirements:

element.short: Human-readable and computation-friendly name for a filter parameter usable by subscriptions on this topic, via Subscription.filterBy.filterParameter
element.definition: Either the canonical URL to a search parameter (like "http://hl7.org/fhir/SearchParameter/encounter-patient") or topic-defined parameter (like "hub.event") which is a label for the filter.
element.comment: Chained parameters are allowed (like "patient.gender") - but can not use colons or modifiers.

element.short: Canonical URL for a filterParameter definition
element.definition: Either the canonical URL to a search parameter (like "http://hl7.org/fhir/SearchParameter/encounter-patient") or the officially-defined URI for a shared filter concept (like "http://example.org/concepts/shared-common-event").
element.comment: Chained parameters are allowed (like "patient.gender") - but can not use colons or modifiers.
